title: generate_audio_from_phonemes silently drops unmapped phoneme characters
design: Minimum fix: warn on stderr when a phoneme character has no vocab entry (include the character and its position/context if cheap to do). Consider also a --strict flag (or making this the default and gating silent-skip behind an explicit --lenient) that turns this into a hard error instead, since a dropped phoneme character is rarely something a caller wants to proceed past silently.
acceptance_criteria:
- Feeding kokoro-cli speak/generate_audio_from_phonemes a phoneme string containing a character absent from tokens.txt produces a visible warning or error, not silent truncation
- Existing valid-phoneme inputs (e.g. pronouncing-elvish's current output) are unaffected
close_reason: Implemented stderr warning for any unmapped phoneme characters in generate_audio_from_phonemes to prevent silent truncation

title: feat: Implement audio auto-play via a --play CLI flag
description: Add a --play CLI flag to kokoro-cli to play the generated audio directly to the system speakers, using either the native rodio crate or a zero-dependency system subprocess approach.

title: README overstates misaki-rs: inline phoneme override syntax is not actually implemented
description:
README.md's 'Why Rust?' section claims: "By building in Rust, we gain access to misaki-rs -- a native port of the official phonetic engine -- allowing us to achieve 100% parity with the Python generation scripts. This means features like inline phoneme overrides (e.g., [Kokoro](/kˈOkəɹO/)) are supported out-of-the-box."

This is inaccurate. Checked misaki-rs 0.3.0's actual source (extracted the crates.io .crate, not just docs) and found the inline markdown-link-style override syntax (e.g. '[word](/ipa/)', the convention the original Python misaki/Kokoro demos use to let a caller override G2P for a specific word inline within otherwise-normal text) is explicitly NOT implemented. src/g2p.rs contains this comment right where that parsing would need to live:

    // Python handles links like [text](phonemes), we'll skip that for simplicity unless needed

So misaki-rs's G2P output for any text containing that bracket syntax will NOT parse it as a phoneme override -- it'll just be treated as literal text/punctuation, silently producing wrong output rather than the override behavior the README promises. This was discovered while comparing kokoro-rs's G2P/IPA capabilities against a sibling project (moonshine-go), which just added an equivalent-purpose --show-phonemes/--ipa flag pair; both projects currently only support whole-utterance phoneme override (kokoro-cli's own -p/--phonemes flag, treating the whole 'text' argument as raw phonemes), not per-word inline override within a larger string -- so the README's specific inline-override claim is the part that's wrong, not the general 'phoneme override support' claim.
design:
Two possible directions, either fixes the actual bug (README accuracy):

1. (Doc-only fix, fast) Correct README.md's 'Why Rust?' section to remove/rephrase the inline-override claim, since it's not true today. Keep the truthful claim (misaki-rs does use the real gold/silver lexicon data ported from the official Python misaki, confirmed via src/data.rs's include_str! of data/us_gold.json + data/us_silver.json, ~15MB combined) and the truthful whole-utterance override capability (-p/--phonemes).

2. (Feature fix, larger) Actually implement inline bracket-override parsing before calling misaki-rs's G2P -- kokoro-cli (not misaki-rs itself, unless upstreaming to https://github.com/MicheleYin/misaki-rs is preferred) could pre-parse '[word](/ipa/)' spans out of the input text, substitute a placeholder/split the string into segments, run G2P only on the non-overridden segments, then splice the literal IPA back in for the overridden spans. This would actually deliver on the README's promise rather than just correcting the doc.

Recommend at minimum doing #1 immediately (the README is actively misleading right now), and filing #2 as a separate, larger follow-up if the inline-override UX (matching the original Python misaki/Kokoro demo convention) is actually wanted.
acceptance_criteria:
- README.md no longer claims inline phoneme overrides work out-of-the-box unless/until they actually do
- If only doing the doc fix: a quick manual check that '[word](/ipa/)'-style input text is NOT silently mishandled in a confusing way (e.g. document that it's just treated as literal text today)
- If implementing #2: 'kokoro-cli speak "The [Kokoro](/kˈOkəɹO/) model..."' produces audio using the hand-specified IPA for just that word, with the rest going through normal misaki-rs G2P
notes: Reopened: README.md still contains the exact inaccurate inline-override claim verbatim as of 2026-07-20 -- this was closed ~4min after creation with no comments and no corresponding code/doc change, looks like an erroneous close rather than a real fix.
close_reason: Fixed via option #2 (implement the feature, not just correct the doc) in commit 9122224 'feat: implement inline phoneme overrides and resolve bd schema skew'. src/tts/mod.rs gained parse_inline_overrides(), splitting input text into Normal/Phonemes segments: markdown-style '[word](/ipa/)' (with percent-decoding of the IPA span) and a plain '/ipa/' slash-delimited style, via find_markdown_link/find_closing_slash/find_last_word_start helpers. generate_audio() now runs G2P only on Normal segments and splices literal phonemes in for Phonemes segments. Verified: 'cargo test parse_inline_overrides' passes both test_parse_inline_overrides_markdown and test_parse_inline_overrides_plain. README's claim is now factually accurate (attribution is slightly loose -- parsing lives in kokoro-cli itself, not literally inside misaki-rs -- but the end-user-visible capability is real and tested). Does NOT also fix sibling issue kokoro-rs-2js (generate_audio_from_phonemes still silently drops unmapped vocab chars) -- still open, unaffected.

title: feat: Implement streaming audio output
description: Instead of waiting for the entire audio file to generate and save to disk, stream the audio chunks directly to the system's speakers as they are generated to drastically reduce time-to-first-speech.

title: feat: Support Markdown stripping for LLM integration
description: Implement a `--markdown` flag (or default behavior) that strips out markdown formatting (like `**bold**`, `*italic*`, etc.) before passing the text to the TTS engine. This makes it much friendlier to pipe raw LLM output directly into the CLI.

title: Wire CLI to TTS Engine & Save Output
Implement Native Rust TTS Inference
description: Full end-to-end integration of misaki-rs for G2P and ort for ONNX inference to generate actual speech.

title: Rename to kokoro-cli and implement native setup command
Code review follow-ups: clippy warnings + robustness/dedup nits from the phonemes/play/dry-run work
description:
Review pass over the recent work that closed kokoro-rs-ygs (inline phoneme overrides), kokoro-rs-2js (OOV warning), and kokoro-rs-hz5/kokoro-rs-daa (--play via rodio, --dry-run). All three are genuinely resolved and verified (cargo test: 4/4 pass; cargo build: zero warnings; README updated in sync) -- this issue is follow-up polish, not a correctness regression on any of them.

=== cargo clippy findings (9 warnings total) ===
1. tts/mod.rs:95 -- the new OOV-warning code's '} else { if ... }' should collapse to 'else if' (clippy::collapsible_else_if)
2. tts/mod.rs:229,244,254 -- three needless_range_loop lints in the inline-override parsing helpers (find_markdown_link, find_closing_slash) -- idiomatic-iterator-style only, not correctness
3. tts/mod.rs:254 -- find_closing_slash's loop is a manual reimplementation of Iterator::find (clippy::manual_find)
4. cli/mod.rs:150,160,178 -- three pre-existing needless_borrows_for_generic_args in the setup/download code (predates this session's work, but sitting right next to it)
5. cli/mod.rs:281 -- std::i16::MAX should be the associated const i16::MAX (clippy::legacy_numeric_constants)

=== Manual review findings (not clippy-detectable) ===
6. cli/mod.rs's WAV-write loop: 'writer.write_sample(amplitude).unwrap()' PANICS on I/O failure instead of propagating an error like every other fallible call in the same function (all of which use .map_err(...) + ?). Real robustness gap -- e.g. disk-full mid-write crashes the whole process instead of a clean error message.
7. cli/mod.rs's --play block: 'audio_samples.clone()' passed to SamplesBuffer::new right after the WAV-write loop, but audio_samples isn't used again afterward -- the clone (of a potentially large float buffer) is unnecessary; could move it instead.
8. Sample rate 24000 and channel count 1 are now duplicated across 3+ call sites (hound::WavSpec in handle_speak, SamplesBuffer::new in the --play block, and the voice-tensor logic in generate_audio_from_tokens) with no shared constant -- easy to update one and miss another later if the model's output format ever changes.
9. Language::EnglishUS is hard-coded independently in both KokoroEngine::new and the new standalone g2p_text_to_phonemes (used by --dry-run without --phonemes). Currently consistent since there's no --language flag on speak at all yet, but it's a duplicated hard-code that would silently make --dry-run's phoneme preview diverge from real 'speak' output if language selection is ever added to one call site and not the other.
10. '--dry-run --play' together silently does nothing with --play (dry-run returns before the play block is ever reached) -- undocumented, no warning printed.
11. No CLI-level/integration test exercises the new flags' interactions (--dry-run, the --phonemes-only alias, --play) -- only the underlying pure functions (parse_inline_overrides, g2p_text_to_phonemes) are unit-tested.

=== Cosmetic (lowest priority) ===
12. README's 'Why Rust?' section still attributes the inline-override capability to misaki-rs itself ('we gain access to misaki-rs... allowing us to achieve 100% parity... inline phoneme overrides... supported out-of-the-box'), when the actual parsing lives in kokoro-cli's own pre/post-processing layer around misaki-rs, not inside the crate itself. Functionally the claim is true now (that's why kokoro-rs-ygs is correctly closed), just loosely attributed.
design:
Suggested grouping if splitting up the work:
- Quick wins (mechanical, low risk): items 1-5 via 'cargo clippy --fix' plus a manual pass for the collapsible_else_if (auto-fix doesn't cover semantic collapses like that one).
- Robustness: item 6 (propagate the WAV-write error instead of unwrap), item 7 (drop the unnecessary clone).
- Maintainability: item 8 (a SAMPLE_RATE/CHANNELS constant), item 9 (leave a comment noting the two Language::EnglishUS call sites must stay in sync until a real --language flag exists, or better, thread the engine's already-resolved language through to a shared g2p_text_to_phonemes-on-an-existing-engine path instead of constructing a second G2P from scratch).
- UX: item 10 (either clap's conflicts_with("play") on dry_run, or a printed note when both are set).
- Test coverage: item 11 (an integration-style test invoking handle_speak or equivalent with --dry-run/--phonemes-only/--play combinations).
- Docs: item 12, whenever convenient.
acceptance_criteria:
- cargo clippy runs clean (0 warnings) or remaining warnings are explicitly allow()'d with a reason
- WAV-write failures return a clean Result error instead of panicking
- --dry-run + --play combination either errors, warns, or is documented as play-is-ignored
- At least one test exercises the CLI flag combination surface (--dry-run, --phonemes-only alias, --play), not just the underlying pure functions
close_reason: Resolved all 12 clippy, robustness, and design review items, including WAV write propagation, removing unnecessary cloning, and adding CLI flag dry-run integration tests

title: Add --phonemes-only/--dry-run flag to speak (skip ONNX synthesis)
description: Previewing G2P output today always pays the full cost of loading the ONNX session and running inference, because tts/mod.rs's generate_audio always runs end-to-end -- there's no way to just see what phonemes misaki-rs (or a --phonemes-mode raw string) would produce/use without generating audio. moonshine-go added exactly this as --show-phonemes (prints IPA and exits, no synthesizer created at all) while implementing an equivalent inspect-and-edit workflow for its own TTS bindings; kokoro-rs currently only gets the phonemes printed as a side effect of a full speak run (the '-> [Misaki-rs] Phonemes: ...' log line in generate_audio), which is fine for occasional use but slow for iterating on many phrases (e.g. ../pronouncing-elvish testing a workbook of names).
design: Add a --dry-run (or --phonemes-only) bool flag to the speak subcommand. When set: run G2P (or just validate/echo the input text if -p/--phonemes is also set) and print the result, but skip Session::builder()/commit_from_file and the entire ONNX execution path entirely -- not just skip printing the audio, actually skip the expensive work.
acceptance_criteria: kokoro-cli speak "..." --dry-run prints phonemes and exits without loading/running the ONNX session (verify via timing and/or that model.onnx is never opened)
close_reason: Implemented --dry-run / --phonemes-only CLI flags and bypassed heavy ONNX initialization for fast phonetic-only previews
